title: Train a time-to-next-stop auxiliary model
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work uses multi-task heads for both next-lap classification and laps-until-stop regression, and discrete-time survival modeling treats each lap as a hazard step.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work uses multi-task heads for both next-lap classification and laps-until-stop regression, and discrete-time survival modeling treats each lap as a hazard step. That matches this target better than a single independent binary classifier.
implementation_hint: From train sequences, derive LapsToNextPit, StopWithin3, StopWithin5, and censored-to-finish flags. Train auxiliary regressors/classifiers or a discrete hazard model, then feed OOF predictions into AutoGluon or blend them with the main probability.
expected_effect: May sharpen ranking around the one-to-five-lap decision window where AUC is won, while adding model diversity beyond static tabular features.
risk: Needs strict group-aware OOF generation to avoid future leakage; noisy sequence boundaries can corrupt the derived labels.
